tasks.title: Disable structlog Rich-traceback show_locals globally to prevent DSN/secret leaks in error logs
acceptance: 1) app/core/logging.py configures structlog's traceback formatter with show_locals=False (or installs a frame-locals redaction processor that scrubs keys matching password|pwd|token|secret|api_key|encrypted_secret|dsn|database_url|connection_string and values matching DSN-like substrings). 2) New test in backend/tests/test_logging.py exercises a real exception with sensitive locals (e.g. asyncpg auth failure with a fake DSN) and asserts that the rendered log line does NOT contain the secret value, the host, the port, the user, or the password key value. 3) ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING toggle still works correctly. 4) ruff + mypy clean. 5) Regression: existing 8 health tests + 39 dep-smoke tests still pass. 6) Existing /ready 503 path continues to log error_class + db_detail (sanitized) without exc_info=True OR with a configuration that no longer leaks.
verification_commands: Real reproduction: launch uvicorn with bad DATABASE_URL (port without listener), curl /ready, grep stdout for 'password', 'pwd', the actual password value, host, port — all must be absent. Run with both ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true and =false. Also reproduce with a deliberate failing path elsewhere (any other use case that catches an exception and logs with exc_info=True). Capture logs in evidence dir.
tasks.target: Disable structlog Rich-traceback show_locals globally to prevent DSN/secret leaks in error logs
tasks.target_raw: Disable structlog Rich-traceback show_locals globally to prevent DSN/secret leaks in error logs

tasks.title: ensure_jwt_keypair: auto-generate dev RSA keypair in setup-from-scratch.sh
acceptance: After 'rm .env, bash scripts/setup-from-scratch.sh' on a fresh clone, .env contains a real RSA keypair (PEM blocks with -----BEGIN/-----END markers, valid 2048+ bits) for JWT_PRIVATE_KEY and JWT_PUBLIC_KEY, AND backend startup loads them without error AND POST /api/v1/auth/sign-up issues a token signable by JWT_PRIVATE_KEY and verifiable by JWT_PUBLIC_KEY end-to-end. Idempotent: re-running setup-from-scratch.sh on an existing valid keypair leaves it untouched (matcher logic analogous to ensure_encryption_key).
verification_commands: 1) rm .env, rm -f .keys/jwt_*.pem (or wherever the new function stores them), 2) bash scripts/setup-from-scratch.sh, 3) grep -E '^JWT_PRIVATE_KEY=' .env | head -1 → must contain a real PEM (or path to one), 4) bash scripts/dev-restart.sh --reset, 5) curl -X POST /api/v1/auth/sign-up with a real test user, expect 201 + JWT in response, 6) Decode the JWT header (jwt.io or jwt-cli) and confirm RS256 algorithm, 7) Re-run setup-from-scratch.sh, confirm idempotency log line.
tasks.target: ensure_jwt_keypair: auto-generate dev RSA keypair in setup-from-scratch.sh
tasks.target_raw: ensure_jwt_keypair: auto-generate dev RSA keypair in setup-from-scratch.sh

tasks.title: Add bash -n .env.example pre-commit/CI lint to prevent placeholder regression
acceptance: Pre-commit hook (or CI lint job, or both) executes 'bash -n .env.example' on every PR/commit that touches .env.example, fails the check on non-zero exit. Hook config is committed to the repo so it is active for all developers without manual setup.
verification_commands: 1) Introduce a deliberate regression in .env.example (e.g. NEWVAR=<broken>) on a feature branch, 2) Run pre-commit (or push and watch CI), 3) Confirm the check fails with non-zero exit and a clear message, 4) Revert the regression and confirm the check passes again.
tasks.target: Add bash -n .env.example pre-commit/CI lint to prevent placeholder regression
tasks.target_raw: Add bash -n .env.example pre-commit/CI lint to prevent placeholder regression

tasks.title: Fix .env DATABASE_URL host port + pydantic env_file path resolution for native dev workflow
acceptance: (1) DATABASE_URL host:port aligned to hilo-postgres exposure (localhost:5433 for native dev or postgres:5432 for compose), .env.example documents both modes clearly. (2) Settings env_file resolves robustly regardless of cwd (e.g. absolute path resolution from the project root, or env_file as a list with project-root anchor, or pydantic-settings model_config.env_file with explicit Path). (3) Cold-boot native uvicorn from project root + native pytest from cd backend/ both report /ready 200 against hilo-postgres. (4) dev-restart.sh --reset succeeds end-to-end on a clean checkout. (5) test_ready_returns_200_when_db_ok turns from FAIL to PASS without any test-side change.
verification_commands: Run from a fresh shell with no env vars set: 1) bash scripts/dev-restart.sh --reset succeeds. 2) cd backend && pytest tests/test_health.py::test_ready_returns_200_when_db_ok passes. 3) Cold-boot uvicorn from project root: curl /ready returns 200 with checks.db.status=ok. 4) Run pytest from project root AND cd backend/ — Settings.database_url loaded with the right credentials in both cases.
tasks.target: Fix .env DATABASE_URL host port + pydantic env_file path resolution for native dev workflow
tasks.target_raw: Fix .env DATABASE_URL host port + pydantic env_file path resolution for native dev workflow

tasks.title: audit_logs: add ip, user_agent, request_id columns (non-negotiable audit fields)
acceptance: NEW migration 0002 adds nullable ip (INET), user_agent (TEXT), request_id (TEXT), resource (TEXT) columns to audit_logs, migration 0001 file remains untouched (immutable post-commit 99d555b), round-trip 0001 -> 0002 -> 0001 -> 0002 exit 0, AuditLog ORM model in auth.py exposes the new columns (Mapped[str | None] etc.), auth service populates them on every sensitive action via X-Request-ID middleware + request.client + request.headers['User-Agent'], integration test verifies non-null values in audit_logs rows written by auth endpoints
verification_commands: alembic upgrade head -> 0002 (head), alembic downgrade -1 -> 0001 (preserves audit rows

new cols dropped), alembic upgrade head -> 0002 again, no errors, integration test inserts audit_log row via auth endpoint and verifies ip / user_agent / request_id / resource are non-null, psql shows nullable=YES on the 4 new columns (backward-compat for any pre-existing rows that may exist by then)
tasks.target: audit_logs: add ip, user_agent, request_id columns (non-negotiable audit fields)
tasks.target_raw: audit_logs: add ip, user_agent, request_id columns (non-negotiable audit fields)

tasks.title: Planner write_set should include sibling migrations when inserting a new Alembic revision N between existing revisions
acceptance: When a planner builds a task pack for a new Alembic revision N that lands between existing revisions A→C, the write_set automatically includes C (the file whose down_revision must shift from A to N). Verified by re-planning a synthetic case.
verification_commands: Add unit test in .claude/bin/tests/ that simulates planning a slice that inserts a new migration mid-chain, assert generated write_set includes the sibling whose down_revision needs updating.
tasks.target: Planner write_set should include sibling migrations when inserting a new Alembic revision N between existing revisions
tasks.target_raw: Planner write_set should include sibling migrations when inserting a new Alembic revision N between existing revisions

tasks.title: MFA enrollment endpoint missing — POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/enroll (TOTP secret generation)
acceptance: POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/enroll generates a real TOTP secret, encrypts it, persists the row in mfa_totp_secrets, returns otpauth_url + base64 QR. Real integration test verifies the row is decrypt-roundtrip equal to the original. Subsequent T006 verify against the same secret + a real pyotp.TOTP code returns 200.
verification_commands: real curl: POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/enroll → 201 with otpauth_url, psql confirms mfa_totp_secrets row exists with non-null secret_encrypted, decrypt_secret returns a 32-byte secret, pyotp.TOTP(secret).now() generates a code that POST /api/v1/auth/2fa/verify accepts

tasks.title: SQLAlchemy echo leaks argon2 hash in verbose logs (CWE-532)
acceptance: ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true must NOT emit raw password_hash values in any logger output. SQLAlchemy echo either disabled, restricted to non-INSERT/UPDATE on auth tables, or password_hash field redacted at logging layer. Test: grep '$argon2id$' on backend stdout during a sign-up flow returns 0.
verification_commands: Run /verify-slice on a future auth slice with ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING=true, assert grep '$argon2id$' on back.log returns 0 across happy + duplicate paths.
tasks.target: SQLAlchemy echo leaks argon2 hash in verbose logs (CWE-532)
tasks.target_raw: SQLAlchemy echo leaks argon2 hash in verbose logs (CWE-532)
